message:
fix(search): split prefixes on non-alphanumeric chars for punctuation handling

Previously, generate_prefixes() used split_whitespace() to tokenize text
before generating edge n-gram prefixes. This meant that words joined by
punctuation (e.g., "hello,world" or "rust:async") were treated as single
tokens, making individual words unsearchable.

Changed to split(|c: char| !c.is_alphanumeric()) which properly handles:
- Comma-separated words: "hello,world" -> ["hello", "world"]
- Colon-delimited terms: "rust:async" -> ["rust", "async"]
- Hyphenated words: "memory-safe" -> ["memory", "safe"]
- Any other punctuation delimiters

This ensures users can find content by searching for individual words
regardless of how they were originally connected in the source text.

Also updates related tests:
- Adds test_generate_prefixes_punctuation to verify punctuation handling
- Improves test data clarity by making tweet text more distinguishable
  (e.g., "tweet text" -> "tweet 42" to match ID in assertions)
- Minor whitespace cleanup in test code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
